api/obj/api/PlcComm.KvHostLink.KvHostLin.title: Class KvHostLinkClientExtensions | KEYENCE Host Link .NET Documentation
api/obj/api/PlcComm.KvHostLink.KvHostLin.keywords: Class KvHostLinkClientExtensions Namespace PlcComm.KvHostLink Assembly PlcComm.KvHostLink.dll High-level helper API for KvHostLinkClient and QueuedKvHostLinkClient. public static class KvHostLinkClientExtensions Inheritance object KvHostLinkClientExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks These extension methods are the recommended user-facing surface for normal application code. They wrap the token-oriented low-level client API with typed reads and writes, bit-in-word helpers, named snapshots, polling, and one-step connection setup. Overloads for QueuedKvHostLinkClient keep compound helper operations exclusive when a shared connection is used. Methods OpenAndConnectAsync(string, int, CancellationToken) Creates a queued client and opens the connection. public static Task<QueuedKvHostLinkClient> OpenAndConnectAsync(string host, int port = 8501, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters host string PLC IP address or hostname. port int KV Host Link TCP/UDP port. Defaults to 8501. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<QueuedKvHostLinkClient> A connected queued client that is safe to share across async callers. Remarks This is the recommended convenience entry point for high-level application code that does not need to construct KvHostLinkConnectionOptions manually. PollAsync(KvHostLinkClient, IEnumerable<string>, TimeSpan, CancellationToken) Continuously polls the specified addresses and yields a snapshot each cycle. public static IAsyncEnumerable<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> PollAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, IEnumerable<string> addresses, TimeSpan interval, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. addresses IEnumerable<string> Address strings in the same format as ReadNamedAsync(KvHostLinkClient, IEnumerable<string>, CancellationToken). interval TimeSpan Time between polls. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token to stop polling. Returns IAsyncEnumerable<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> Remarks If the address set is batchable, the compiled read plan is reused on every iteration for lower per-cycle overhead. PollAsync(QueuedKvHostLinkClient, IEnumerable<string>, TimeSpan, CancellationToken) Continuously polls the specified addresses and yields a snapshot each cycle. public static IAsyncEnumerable<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> PollAsync(this QueuedKvHostLinkClient client, IEnumerable<string> addresses, TimeSpan interval, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client QueuedKvHostLinkClient addresses IEnumerable<string> interval TimeSpan ct CancellationToken Returns IAsyncEnumerable<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> ReadDWordsAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 32-bit values starting at device. public static Task<uint[]> ReadDWordsAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. device string Starting device address (for example "DM0"). count int Number of 32-bit values to read. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<uint[]> Unsigned 32-bit values in logical device order. Remarks This helper preserves single-request semantics by delegating to ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken). ReadDWordsAsync(QueuedKvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 32-bit values starting at device. public static Task<uint[]> ReadDWordsAsync(this QueuedKvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client QueuedKvHostLinkClient device string count int ct CancellationToken Returns Task<uint[]> ReadDWordsChunkedAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 32-bit values using explicit chunking. public static Task<uint[]> ReadDWordsChunkedAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, int maxDwordsPerRequest, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient Connected Host Link client. device string Start device address. count int Number of 32-bit values to read. maxDwordsPerRequest int Maximum double-words per protocol request. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<uint[]> The concatenated 32-bit values from all explicit chunks. Remarks Chunking is opt-in and advances only by whole double-word boundaries. ReadDWordsChunkedAsync(QueuedKvHostLinkClient, string, int, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 32-bit values using explicit chunking. public static Task<uint[]> ReadDWordsChunkedAsync(this QueuedKvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, int maxDwordsPerRequest, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client QueuedKvHostLinkClient device string count int maxDwordsPerRequest int ct CancellationToken Returns Task<uint[]> ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 32-bit values using one protocol request or returns an error. public static Task<uint[]> ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient Connected Host Link client. device string Start device address. count int Number of 32-bit values to read. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<uint[]> The contiguous 32-bit values read by one request. Remarks Use this helper when the logical range must stay atomic. ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync(QueuedKvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 32-bit values using one protocol request or returns an error. public static Task<uint[]> ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync(this QueuedKvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client QueuedKvHostLinkClient device string count int ct CancellationToken Returns Task<uint[]> ReadNamedAsync(KvHostLinkClient, IEnumerable<string>, CancellationToken) Reads multiple named values and returns a snapshot dictionary. public static Task<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> ReadNamedAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, IEnumerable<string> addresses, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. addresses IEnumerable<string> Address strings that specify both the base device and the desired interpretation. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> A dictionary keyed by the original input address strings. Remarks Address format examples: "DM100" -- unsigned 16-bit (ushort) "DM100:F" -- float "DM100:S" -- signed 16-bit (short) "DM100:D" -- unsigned 32-bit "DM100:L" -- signed 32-bit "DM100.3" -- bit 3 within word (bool) "DM100.A" -- bit 10 within word (bool); bits 10-15 use hex digits A-F Bit-in-word indices use hexadecimal notation (0-F), matching the KEYENCE address format. Bits 0-9 can be written as decimal digits; bits 10-15 must be written as A-F. For example, bit 12 is addressed as "DM100.C", not "DM100.12". When all requested addresses are compatible with helper-layer batching, this method merges contiguous reads into one or more RDS operations. Mixed or non-optimizable address sets fall back to sequential helper reads with the same return shape. ReadNamedAsync(QueuedKvHostLinkClient, IEnumerable<string>, CancellationToken) Reads multiple named values and returns a snapshot dictionary. public static Task<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> ReadNamedAsync(this QueuedKvHostLinkClient client, IEnumerable<string> addresses, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client QueuedKvHostLinkClient addresses IEnumerable<string> ct CancellationToken Returns Task<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> ReadTypedAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, string, CancellationToken) Reads a single device value and converts it to a high-level CLR type. public static Task<object> ReadTypedAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, string dtype, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. device string Base device address string, for example "DM100". dtype string High-level data type code: "U" = ushort, "S" = short, "D" = uint, "L" = signed 32-bit int, "F" = IEEE 754 float32. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<object> A boxed CLR value. Integer formats return boxed integral types and "F" returns a boxed float. Remarks The float helper is implemented at the extension layer by reading two consecutive .U words and combining them as low-word, high-word. ReadTypedAsync(QueuedKvHostLinkClient, string, string, CancellationToken) Reads a single device value and converts it to a high-level CLR type. public static Task<object> ReadTypedAsync(this QueuedKvHostLinkClient client, string device, string dtype, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client QueuedKvHostLinkClient device string dtype string ct CancellationToken Returns Task<object> ReadWordsAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 16-bit words starting at device. public static Task<ushort[]> ReadWordsAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. device string Starting device address (e.g. "DM0"). count int Number of words to read. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<ushort[]> Unsigned word values in PLC order. Remarks This helper is the preferred user-facing block-read API for contiguous word devices. It preserves single-request semantics by delegating to ReadWordsSingleRequestAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken). ReadWordsAsync(QueuedKvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 16-bit words starting at device. public static Task<ushort[]> ReadWordsAsync(this QueuedKvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client QueuedKvHostLinkClient device string count int ct CancellationToken Returns Task<ushort[]> ReadWordsChunkedAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 16-bit words using explicit chunking. public static Task<ushort[]> ReadWordsChunkedAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, int maxWordsPerRequest, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient Connected Host Link client. device string Start device address. count int Number of words to read. maxWordsPerRequest int Maximum words per protocol request. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<ushort[]> The concatenated word values from all explicit chunks. Remarks Chunking is opt-in and advances only by contiguous word boundaries. ReadWordsChunkedAsync(QueuedKvHostLinkClient, string, int, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 16-bit words using explicit chunking. public static Task<ushort[]> ReadWordsChunkedAsync(this QueuedKvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, int maxWordsPerRequest, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client QueuedKvHostLinkClient device string count int maxWordsPerRequest int ct CancellationToken Returns Task<ushort[]> ReadWordsSingleRequestAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 16-bit words using one protocol request or returns an error. public static Task<ushort[]> ReadWordsSingleRequestAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient Connected Host Link client. device string Start device address. count int Number of words to read. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<ushort[]> The contiguous word values read by one request. Remarks Use this helper when the logical range must stay atomic. user/DEVICE_HANDLING.html.title: Device and Type Handling | KEYENCE Host Link .NET Documentation
user/DEVICE_HANDLING.html.keywords: Device and Type Handling This page describes device handling from the recommended high-level API. Typed Helpers ReadTypedAsync and WriteTypedAsync are the standard entry points for single typed values. dtype Meaning Width .NET type U unsigned 16-bit 1 word ushort S signed 16-bit 1 word short D unsigned 32-bit 2 words uint L signed 32-bit 2 words int F IEEE 754 float32 2 words float Example: ushort dm0 = (ushort)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM0", "U"); int counter = (int)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM10", "L"); float temp = (float)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM20", "F"); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM100", "U", dm0); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM110", "L", counter); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM120", "F", temp); Contiguous Areas Use explicit single-request helpers when the data occupies consecutive word addresses and one PLC request is required. ushort[] words = await client.ReadWordsSingleRequestAsync("DM200", 8); uint[] dwords = await client.ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync("DM300", 4); ReadWordsSingleRequestAsync reads contiguous unsigned 16-bit values ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync reads contiguous unsigned 32-bit values from adjacent words Use explicit chunked helpers only when splitting is acceptable: ushort[] largeWords = await client.ReadWordsChunkedAsync("DM1000", 200, maxWordsPerRequest: 64); uint[] largeDwords = await client.ReadDWordsChunkedAsync("DM2000", 40, maxDwordsPerRequest: 32); Bit-in-Word Addresses Use WriteBitInWordAsync to update a single bit inside a word device without disturbing the other bits. await client.WriteBitInWordAsync("DM500", bitIndex: 0, value: true); await client.WriteBitInWordAsync("DM500", bitIndex: 10, value: false); Mixed Snapshots Use ReadNamedAsync when one application snapshot mixes typed word values and bit-in-word values. Supported notation: Format Meaning "DM100" unsigned 16-bit "DM100:S" signed 16-bit "DM100:D" unsigned 32-bit "DM100:L" signed 32-bit "DM100:F" float32 "DM100.3" bit 3 inside the word "DM100.A" bit 10 inside the word Example: var snapshot = await client.ReadNamedAsync( new[] { "DM100", "DM101:S", "DM102:D", "DM104:F", "DM200.3", "DM200.A" }); Bit indices use hexadecimal notation from 0 to F. Scope This user-facing page intentionally stops at the helper layer. Token-oriented reads, raw suffix handling, and direct protocol operations are documented for maintainers only.

user/USER_GUIDE.html.title: User Guide: Host Link Communication .NET | KEYENCE Host Link .NET Documentation
user/USER_GUIDE.html.keywords: User Guide: Host Link Communication .NET This guide covers the recommended high-level API only. Use these entry points in normal application code: KvHostLinkClientFactory.OpenAndConnectAsync KvHostLinkConnectionOptions ReadTypedAsync WriteTypedAsync WriteBitInWordAsync ReadNamedAsync PollAsync ReadWordsSingleRequestAsync ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync ReadWordsChunkedAsync ReadDWordsChunkedAsync KvHostLinkAddress.Normalize Raw token-oriented reads, writes, and protocol details are intentionally left to the maintainer documentation. Installation Add a project reference to: src/PlcComm.KvHostLink/PlcComm.KvHostLink.csproj or reference the compiled assembly from a .NET 9 application. Connect Once using PlcComm.KvHostLink; var options = new KvHostLinkConnectionOptions("192.168.250.100", 8501); await using var client = await KvHostLinkClientFactory.OpenAndConnectAsync(options); The factory returns an open QueuedKvHostLinkClient ready for the helper extension methods below. The shortcut OpenAndConnectAsync(host, port) remains available, but the factory plus options object is the recommended public entry point. Typed Read and Write Supported dtype codes: dtype Meaning Width U unsigned 16-bit 1 word S signed 16-bit 1 word D unsigned 32-bit 2 words L signed 32-bit 2 words F IEEE 754 float32 2 words ushort u = (ushort)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM0", "U"); short s = (short)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM1", "S"); uint d = (uint)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM2", "D"); float f = (float)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM4", "F"); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM10", "U", u); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM11", "S", s); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM12", "D", d); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM14", "F", f); F is implemented in the helper layer by converting two .U words as float32. Contiguous Blocks Use explicit single-request helpers when one PLC request is required. ushort[] words = await client.ReadWordsSingleRequestAsync("DM100", 8); uint[] dwords = await client.ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync("DM200", 4); Use explicit chunked helpers only when splitting is acceptable: ushort[] largeWords = await client.ReadWordsChunkedAsync("DM1000", 200, maxWordsPerRequest: 64); uint[] largeDwords = await client.ReadDWordsChunkedAsync("DM2000", 40, maxDwordsPerRequest: 32); *SingleRequestAsync does not silently split one logical request. If the PLC or protocol cannot satisfy that request shape, the helper returns an error. Bit in Word Use WriteBitInWordAsync for bit updates inside word devices such as DM, EM, FM, W, or Z. await client.WriteBitInWordAsync("DM500", bitIndex: 0, value: true); await client.WriteBitInWordAsync("DM500", bitIndex: 3, value: false); This helper performs a read-modify-write so that the other 15 bits remain unchanged. Mixed Snapshots with ReadNamedAsync Supported address notation: Format Meaning "DM100" unsigned 16-bit "DM100:S" signed 16-bit "DM100:D" unsigned 32-bit "DM100:L" signed 32-bit "DM100:F" float32 "DM100.3" bit 3 inside the word "DM100.A" bit 10 inside the word var snapshot = await client.ReadNamedAsync( new[] { "DM100", "DM101:S", "DM102:D", "DM104:F", "DM200.0", "DM200.A" }); Bit indices use hexadecimal notation from 0 to F. Address Normalization Use KvHostLinkAddress.Normalize when you want one stable string form: string canonical = KvHostLinkAddress.Normalize("dm100.a"); Console.WriteLine(canonical); // DM100.A Polling PollAsync repeatedly yields the same kind of snapshot dictionary. using var cts = new CancellationTokenSource(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(10)); await foreach (var snapshot in client.PollAsync( new[] { "DM100", "DM101:L", "DM200.3" }, TimeSpan.FromSeconds(1), cts.Token)) { Console.WriteLine(snapshot["DM100"]); } Error Handling Exception Meaning HostLinkError PLC returned an error code HostLinkProtocolError Local validation or parsing failure HostLinkConnectionError Connect, disconnect, socket, or timeout failure try { var value = await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM100", "U"); } catch (HostLinkProtocolError ex) { Console.WriteLine($"Protocol error: {ex.Message}"); } catch (HostLinkConnectionError ex) { Console.WriteLine($"Connection error: {ex.Message}"); } catch (HostLinkError ex) { Console.WriteLine($"PLC error: {ex.Message}"); } Recommended Samples API / workflow Sample Purpose KvHostLinkClientFactory.OpenAndConnectAsync, KvHostLinkConnectionOptions, KvHostLinkAddress.Normalize, ReadTypedAsync, WriteTypedAsync, ReadWordsSingleRequestAsync, ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync, ReadWordsChunkedAsync, ReadDWordsChunkedAsync, WriteBitInWordAsync, ReadNamedAsync, PollAsync samples/PlcComm.KvHostLink.HighLevelSample/PlcComm.KvHostLink.HighLevelSample.csproj Full helper-layer walkthrough KvHostLinkClientFactory.OpenAndConnectAsync, KvHostLinkConnectionOptions, ReadTypedAsync, WriteTypedAsync, ReadWordsSingleRequestAsync, ReadDWordsSingleRequestAsync samples/PlcComm.KvHostLink.BasicReadWriteSample/PlcComm.KvHostLink.BasicReadWriteSample.csproj Focused typed and contiguous single-request example ReadNamedAsync, WriteBitInWordAsync, PollAsync samples/PlcComm.KvHostLink.NamedPollingSample/PlcComm.KvHostLink.NamedPollingSample.csproj Mixed snapshot and polling example
